key_insight: Spray method strongly controls coating quality: HVOF is better for carbide coatings (WC-NiCr, Cr3C2-NiCr), while detonation spraying is better for Al2O3-NiCr.
engineering_relevance: Selecting the deposition process by coating chemistry can improve hardness, microstructure density, and wear performance in surface-engineered components.
method: Compared WC-NiCr, Cr3C2-NiCr, and Al2O3-NiCr coatings made by HVOF and detonation spraying.
Characterized powder and coating morphology with SEM and EDS.
Measured microhardness and tribology using ASTM-based ball-on-disc testing (Anton Paar TRB3).
evidence_signal: The abstract reports comparative SEM/EDS, hardness, and friction/wear evaluations showing denser, finer structures and better tribology for carbide coatings via HVOF, and higher hardness for Al2O3-NiCr via detonation spraying.
watch_item: Results are system-specific, so process optimization may not transfer directly to different powders, substrates, or spray parameters without revalidation.
paper_title: <b>Comparative analysis of WC-NiCr, AL₂O₃-NiCr, and CR₃C₂-NiCr metal-ceramic coatings applied by HVOF and detonation spraying methods</b>
